attempts.finding: After adding tracer mixed-content childText capture and re-running the tracer, the canonical textarea renders width 186 while the native Vue textarea renders 187 (deterministic 1px, all viewports). Placeholder text 'Write a message' is visually identical (image-verified); diff is ~161 px confined to placeholder glyph edges from the 1px width shift. Within the <=1px meaningful-part geometry tolerance but fails the exact-pixel tier on default/disabled (placeholder-bearing) states.
attempts.hypothesis: textarea intrinsic width is content/placeholder-driven and shows tracer-vs-native 1px font-metrics variance, the same class as input placeholder rendering. Not a structural defect; markup is byte-identical.
attempts.nextApproach: verify tracer determinism for textarea width across repeat traces; if canonical is stable at 186 and native stable at 187, this is a sub-pixel placeholder-text tier question to surface, not an ordinary bug.
